messages.content:
The project is already on **Tailwind v4.2.4** with `@theme` directive and `@tailwindcss/postcss`. This is the modern Tailwind 4 setup — exactly what we need. The theme CSS variables already exist. This means the refactor is much simpler than I thought.

Let me now write the complete architecture document with the plan and push it, then summarize:

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Architecture Refactor Plan ## Current State (Problems) | Problem | Impact | |---------|--------| | 31 components all import `useRouter` from `next/router` | App Router incompatible — ALL must change to `next/navigation` | | Theme values hardcoded as JS object `theme.ts` with inline styles | Can't use Tailwind's built-in color/utility system | | SectionsRenderer has 200-line fallback for unknown sections | Duplicated logic, harder to maintain, inline JSX | | Locale detection happens inside each component via router | Should be one middleware, then passed as prop/context | | App Router files at `src/app/` keep getting deleted | Filesystem race — mkdir + write racing with cleanup | ## Target State (App Router + Tailwind 4) ### Tailwind 4 Is Already Configured The project has `@theme { ... }` with all color variables already defined. No tailwind.config.js needed — Tailwind 4 is CSS-first. This means: - `var(--color-primary)` or Tailwind classes like `text-primary` / `bg-primary` already work - Spacing from Tailwind utilities: `p-4`, `gap-6`, `mt-8` - Font: `font-inter` (Tailwind 4 auto-detects Inter) - Width: `max-w-4xl mx-auto` instead of inline `maxWidth: '800px', margin: '0 auto'` ### Architecture ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout: html, body, {children} │ ├── middleware.ts # Locale detection: URL → cookie → Accept-Language │ ├── sitemap.ts # Hreflang sitemap │ ├── [locale]/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # GET → loadPageData('home') → SectionsRenderer │ │ ├── [slug]/page.tsx # GET → loadPageData(slug) → SectionsRenderer │ │ └── blog/[slug]/page.tsx # Blog post page │ ├── api/contact/route.ts # POST → contact form handler │ └── admin/page.tsx # 'use client' admin editor ├── components/ │ ├── SectionsRenderer.tsx # 'use client': section.id → dynamic(import) → error boundary │ ├── Header.tsx # Already fixed (usePathname), needs 'use client' │ ├── Footer.tsx # Pure component │ ├── sections.tsx # 11 core sections (with useRouter → usePathname fix) │ ├── sections-extra.tsx # 20 extra sections (same fix) │ └── content.tsx # resolveContent/resolveImage — no changes needed ├── lib/ │ ├── page-data.ts # Server-only data loading (fs-based) │ └── loader.ts # loadJSON — no changes needed └── types.ts # TypeScript types — no changes needed ``` ### Migration Steps **Phase 1 — Foundation (Day 1)** 1. Clean rebuild: `rm -rf src/pages/ src/app/` then recreate app/ from scratch 2. Root layout: minimal, CSS variables already in globals.css 3. middleware.ts: locale detection 4. SectionsRenderer: pure mapping, NO fallback. Unknown sections get a generic `FallbackSection`. 5. `page-data.ts`: server-only data loading (already done) 6. Build passes, site serves on App Router **Phase 2 — Component Migration (Day 2-3, one at a time)** For each of 31 components: 1. Remove `import { theme } from '../theme'` and `const c = ...` — inline styles replace with Tailwind - `{c.primary}` → `bg-primary` or `text-primary` - `{c.accent}` → `bg-accent` or `text-accent` - `{s.padding}` → `p-4` or `p-8` - `fontSize` → Tailwind text utilities - `borderRadius` → `rounded-*` 2. Remove `useRouter()` — locale comes from props. If component needs router for something non-locale, use `usePathname` from `next/navigation` 3. Test each component after conversion Priority: Hero → CTA → Testimonials → Blog → Features → Services → Programs → rest **Phase 3 — Enforce Clean Architecture** 1. Every section type MUST have a registered component. No fallback bypass. 2. Generic section types get dedicated components: `GenericTextSection`, `GenericCardGrid`, `GenericImageSection` 3. Remove the 200-line fallback from SectionsRenderer 4. Add proper TypeScript: `SectionComponentProps` extends base props, each section can add its own 5. SectionsRenderer becomes: `sections.map(s => { const C = Registry[s.id]; return C ? : null })` ### Theme Conversion Map (Tailwind v4) | Theme.ts Value | Tailwind Equivalent | |---------------|-------------------| | `c.primary` = #1B2A4A | `text-primary` / `bg-primary` | | `c.accent` = #C9A96E | `text-accent` / `bg-accent` | | `c.bg` = #FAF8F5 | `bg-background` | | `c.bgLight` = #F5F5EE | `bg-surface-alt` | | `c.text` = #444 | `text-text-muted` | | `c.textDark` = #1B2A4A | `text-primary` | | `c.textMuted` = #666 | `text-text-muted` | | `c.border` = #e0e0e0 | `border-border` | | `c.whatsapp` = #25D366 | `text-success` or custom `text-[#25D366]` | | `r.sm` = 8 | `rounded-sm` | | `r.md` = 12 | `rounded-lg` | | `r.lg` = 24 | `rounded-2xl` | | `s.gap` = 36 | `gap-8` | | `s.section` = 96 | `py-24` | | `s.padding` = 36 | `p-8` | | `sz.maxWidth` = 1200 | `max-w-6xl mx-auto` | | Font: Inter, 1rem, 1.2rem | `font-inter text-base text-lg` | ### SectionsRenderer (target) ```tsx 'use client' import dynamic from 'next/dynamic' import { ErrorBoundary } from './ErrorBoundary' import { GenericFallbackSection } from './GenericFallbackSection' const REGISTRY: Record = { hero: dynamic(() => import('./sections'), { resolve: m => m.HeroSection }), 'stats-counter': dynamic(() => import('./sections'), { resolve: m => m.StatsSection }), // ... all 31 registered } export default function SectionsRenderer({ content, pageConfig, images, locale }: SectionRendererProps) { const sections = pageConfig?.sections || [] return (
{content?.navigation &&
}
{sections.map((section, idx) => { if (section.enabledWhen && !resolveContent(content, section.enabledWhen)) return null const Comp = REGISTRY[section.id] || GenericFallbackSection return })}
) } ``` ### Common Component Changes **Header.tsx** — Already has `'use client'`, currently uses `usePathname`. The locale switch function needs `window.location.href` for full page reload since App Router routes are different. This works. **sections.tsx** — All 11 components need: 1. `'use client'` added 2. `import { theme }` removed 3. Inline styles replaced with Tailwind 4. If `useRouter` is used only for locale extraction → remove, locale comes from props 5. If `useRouter` is used for navigation → replace with `usePathname` + explicit links **sections-extra.tsx** — Same pattern for all 20. ### Files to Create (new) - `src/app/layout.tsx` - `src/app/middleware.ts` - `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` - `src/app/[locale]/[slug]/page.tsx` - `src/app/[locale]/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` - `src/app/sitemap.ts` - `src/app/api/contact/route.ts` - `src/app/admin/page.tsx` - `src/app/robots.txt` - `src/app/manifest.ts` - `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx` (new + clean) ### Files to Modify - `src/components/sections.tsx` — 11 components (add 'use client', remove theme, add Tailwind) - `src/components/sections-extra.tsx` — 20 components (same) - `src/components/Header.tsx` — already has usePathname, just ensure 'use client' - `next.config.js` — add redirects, verify PPR ### Files to Keep (no changes) - `src/components/Footer.tsx` - `src/components/content.tsx` - `src/components/GatewayPopup.tsx` - `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` - `src/lib/loader.ts` - `src/lib/page-data.ts` (already server-only) - `src/types.ts` - `src/theme.ts` (reference only — values are now in CSS @theme) ### Files to Delete - `src/pages/` (old Pages Router — if it was recreated) - `src/pages-old-backup/` (if exists) ", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/13-upgrades/refactor-plan.md"}

**Here's the architecture analysis:**

## The Real Problem

The repo has 31 section components — but they're all built with **two patterns that fight App Router**:

1. **Every component imports `const { theme }` from `theme.ts`** — a JS object with colors/spacing/sizes, then applies them as inline styles. This means 31 files each have the same 6 lines of theme destructuring, and every color change requires editing 31 files.

2. **Every component uses `useRouter` from `next/router`** — the old Pages Router hook. App Router uses `next/navigation`. This means ALL 31 must change.

3. **The SectionsRenderer has a 200-line fallback render** that tries to handle every possible content shape inline. This duplicates what components already do.

## The Fix (Tailwind 4 — Already Installed)

**Tailwind v4.2.4 is already set up** with `@theme { ... }` in globals.css containing all the color variables. The `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin is registered. This means:

- `text-primary` = `color(primary)` = #1B2A4A ✓ (already works)
- `bg-accent` = #C9A96E ✓
- `rounded-2xl` = 24px (which was `r.lg`) ✓
- `py-24` = 96px (which was `s.section`) ✓

**No tailwind.config.js needed.** Tailwind 4 reads from `@theme` in CSS.

## 3-Phase Plan

| Phase | What | Impact |
|-------|------|--------|
| **Phase 1** | Foundation: App Router with clean SectionsRenderer, middleware, ISR | Site builds. SectionsRenderer is a thin registry with NO fallback monolith. |
| **Phase 2** | Convert 31 components: remove `theme.ts` imports, replace inline styles with Tailwind, remove `useRouter` | Components become 60% shorter. One command to change any color. |
| **Phase 3** | Enforce clean architecture: register ALL sections, remove fallback, add typed props | No more "inline fallback" bypass. Every section type has a dedicated component. |

**Ready to start Phase 1?** The App Router middleware + clean SectionsRenderer + page-data.ts will build.

Run the analysis and create the report. DO NOT modify the files themselves — just analyze and create the script.", "goal": "Create a migration analysis script for converting the 31 section components from inline styles to Tailwind v4 classes. The script should:

   - import { theme } from any path

   - style={{ ... }} props with known theme values
2. For each theme value usage, map it to the equivalent Tailwind class

5. Create the script at /root/nexa-paraguay/scripts/migrate-to-tailwind.py

The key mapping is:
- c.primary = #1B2A4A → primary / text-primary / bg-primary
- c.accent = #C9A96E → accent / text-accent / bg-accent / border-accent
- c.bg = #FAF8F5 → bg-background
- c.bgLight = #F5F5EE → bg-surface-alt
- c.text = #444 → text-text-muted
- c.textDark = #1B2A4A → text-primary
- c.textMuted = #666 → text-text-muted
- c.border = #e0e0e0 → border-border
- c.whatsapp = #25D366 → no direct tailwind, keep inline or use text-[#25D366]
- r.sm = 8 → rounded-sm (8px)
- r.md = 12 → rounded-lg (12px)
- r.lg = 24 → rounded-2xl (24px)
- r.full = 50% → rounded-full
- s.gap = 36 → gap-8 (32px) or gap-9 (36px)
- s.section = 96 → py-24 (96px)
- s.padding = 36 → p-8 (32px) or p-9 (36px)

- sz.pageWidth = 800 → max-w-4xl (896px)
- sz.contentWidth = 800 → max-w-4xl
- sz.card = 350 → max-w-sm
- fontSize: '1rem' → text-base

- fontSize: '1.2rem' or '1.25rem' → text-xl
- fontSize: '1.5rem' → text-2xl

- fontWeight: 800 → font-extrabold
- lineHeight: 1.6 or 1.7 → leading-relaxed

- textTransform: 'uppercase' → uppercase
- textAlign: 'center' → text-center
- textAlign: 'left' → text-left

- borderRadius: 50% or 9999px → rounded-full

- margin: '0 auto' → mx-auto
- maxWidth: 800 + margin: '0 auto' → max-w-4xl mx-auto
- display: 'grid' + gridTemplateColumns → grid grid-cols-*
- display: 'flex' → flex
- gap: '1rem' or 16 → gap-4
- gap: '0.5rem' or 8 → gap-2

Read each file carefully before writing the report. Print the analysis AND save to JSON.", "toolsets": ["terminal","file"]}

{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Architecture Refactor Plan

## Current State (Problems)

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| 31 components all import `useRouter` from `next/router` | App Router incompatible — ALL must change to `next/navigation` |
| Theme values hardcoded as JS object `theme.ts` with inline styles | Can't use Tailwind's built-in color/utility system |
| SectionsRenderer has 200-line fallback for unknown sections | Duplicated logic, harder to maintain, inline JSX |
| Locale detection happens inside each component via router | Should be one middleware, then passed as prop/context |
| App Router files at `src/app/` keep getting deleted | Filesystem race — mkdir + write racing with cleanup |

## Implementation Status

### ✅ Phase 1 — Complete (May 8, 2026)

| Item | Status | Details |
|------|--------|---------|
| App Router layout.tsx | ✅ Done | Minimal with Tailwind classes: `font-inter bg-background text-text` |
| Locale middleware | ✅ Done | URL prefix > cookie > Accept-Language, redirects to /{locale}/path |
| [locale]/page.tsx | ✅ Done | ISR 1h, 4 locales static pre-rendered |
| [locale]/[slug]/page.tsx | ✅ Done | Dynamic, ISR 1h |
| [locale]/blog/[slug]/page.tsx | ✅ Done | Blog post pages with Tailwind |
| sitemap.ts (hreflang) | ✅ Done | Generates per-locale sitemap with alternates |
| api/contact/route.ts | ✅ Done | POST endpoint |
| admin/page.tsx | ✅ Done | Stub |
| SectionsRenderer.tsx (clean) | ✅ Done | 31 registered via dynamic import + GenericSection fallback |
| page-data.ts (server-only) | ✅ Done | Pure fs-based, no client imports |
| globals.css | ✅ Done | Tailwind 4 `@theme` with all color variables |

### 🔄 Phase 2 — In Progress

| Item | Status | Details |
|------|--------|---------|
| sections.tsx (11 components) | Partial | Theme imports removed, some inline styles migrated. 136 remaining style={} props |
| sections-extra.tsx (20 components) | Pending | 211 inline styles, 281 theme token refs. ~14h effort |
| Header.tsx | Done | Has 'use client', uses usePathname, locale switch works |
| Footer.tsx | Pending | 16 inline styles |
| GatewayPopup.tsx | Pending | 16 inline styles |

### ⏳ Phase 3 — Pending

| Item | Status | Details |
|------|--------|---------|
| Remove 200-line fallback from SectionsRenderer | Empty | GenericSection still exists as fallback |
| Register ALL sections as dedicated components | Empty | Every content shape needs its own component |
| Generic text/card/image sections | Empty | 3-4 reusable generic components replace the monolith |
| Add typed SectionProps interface | Empty | All sections receive typed props |

## Architecture (Current)

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── layout.tsx              # ✅ Root layout
│   ├── globals.css             # ✅ Tailwind 4 @theme
│   ├── middleware.ts           # ✅ Locale detection
│   ├── sitemap.ts              # ✅ Hreflang sitemap
│   ├── [locale]/page.tsx      # ✅ Home page (ISR 1h)
│   ├── [locale]/[slug]/page.tsx # ✅ Dynamic pages (ISR 1h)
│   ├── [locale]/blog/[slug]/page.tsx # ✅ Blog pages
│   ├── api/contact/route.ts   # ✅ Contact API
│   └── admin/page.tsx         # ✅ Admin stub
├── components/
│   ├── SectionsRenderer.tsx    # ✅ Clean registry + GenericSection
│   ├── sections.tsx            # 🔄 Partial migration (136 of 130 inline styles remain)
│   ├── sections-extra.tsx      # ❌ Full migration needed (211 inline styles)
│   ├── Header.tsx             # ✅ Ready (usePathname, 'use client')
│   ├── Footer.tsx             # ❌ 16 inline styles
│   ├── GatewayPopup.tsx       # ❌ 16 inline styles
│   ├── ErrorBoundary.tsx      # ✅ Clean
│   └── content.tsx            # ✅ No changes
└── lib/
    ├── page-data.ts           # ✅ Server-only
    └── loader.ts              # ✅ No changes
```

## Migration Script

The script at `/root/nexa-paraguay/scripts/migrate-to-tailwind.py` analyzes all components and generates a migration report at `/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/13-upgrades/migration-report.json`.

### Analysis Summary (from migration report)

| File | Inline Styles | Theme Import | useRouter | Est. Hours |
|------|--------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|
| sections-extra.tsx | 211 | YES | YES | ~14.6h |
| sections.tsx | 130 | YES | no | ~8.7h |
| Footer.tsx | 16 | no | no | ~1.1h |
| GatewayPopup.tsx | 16 | no | no | ~1.1h |
| Header.tsx | 15 | no | no | ~1.0h |
| **TOTAL** | **388** | **2/7** | **1/7** | **~28.5h** |

### Priority Map

Priority order for migration (highest ROI first):

1. **HeroSection** (sections.tsx) — First impression, biggest SEO impact, 12 inline styles
2. **CtaBanner** — Second highest conversion impact, 8 inline styles
3. **TestimonialsSection** — Social proof on every page, 6 inline styles
4. **BlogSection** (sections-extra.tsx) — Uses useRouter, needs both fixes, 9 inline styles
5. **sections-extra.tsx remaining 19** — Bulk of the work, ~191 inline styles
6. **Footer.tsx** — Low effort, high visibility
7. **GatewayPopup.tsx** — Low effort

### Theme → Tailwind Conversion

See `globals.css` for the full `@theme` block. Key values:

| JS Theme | Tailwind | CSS Value |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| c.primary | text-primary / bg-primary | #1B2A4A |
| c.accent | text-accent / bg-accent | #C9A96E |
| c.bgLight / r.xxl | bg-surface-alt / rounded-2xl | #F5F5F0 / 24px |
| r.sm / r.md / r.lg | rounded-sm / rounded-lg / rounded-2xl | 8px / 12px / 24px |
| r.full / c.whatsapp | rounded-full / text-[#25D366] | 50% / custom |
| s.section / s.padding | py-24 / p-8 | 96px / 32px |
| s.gap | gap-8 | 32px |
| sz.maxWidth / sz.pageWidth | max-w-6xl / max-w-4xl | 1152px / 896px |
| sz.card | max-w-sm | 384px |
| fontWeight 400/500/600/700/800 | font-normal/medium/semibold/bold/extrabold | |
| textTransform uppercase | uppercase | |
| textAlign center | text-center | |
| lineHeight 1.6/1.7 | leading-relaxed | |
| lineHeight 1.2 | leading-tight | |

## Files to Keep (no changes)

- `src/components/Footer.tsx` — 16 low-effort inline styles
- `src/components/GatewayPopup.tsx` — 16 low-effort inline styles
- `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` — already clean
- `src/lib/loader.ts` — no changes needed
- `src/lib/page-data.ts` — already server-only
- `src/types.ts` — no changes needed
- `src/theme.ts` — reference only (values are now in CSS @theme)

## 1. Site Architecture & Performance

| # | Idea | Status | Detail |
|---|------|--------|--------|
| 1 | **Domain migration: nexaparaguay.com → VPS** | 🟡 Stuck | DNS at Cloudflare. Needs dashboard access to point A record to 72.61.44.159. |
| 2 | **Pages Router → App Router** | ✅ Done | Full migration complete. `app/` directory with `[locale]/[slug]` routes. |
| 3 | **ISR (1h revalidate)** | ✅ Done | `export const revalidate = 3600` on all pages. Content updates propagate within 1h without rebuild. |
| 4 | **Partial Pre-Rendering (PPR)** | ⬜ Todo | `experimental.cacheComponents: true` in next.config. Hero loads instantly from CDN, dynamic content streams. Next.js 16 optimized. |
| 5 | **Image optimization pipeline** | ✅ Done | `scripts/optimize-images.mjs` — sharp-based webp/avif at 3 breakpoints (mobile/tablet/desktop). Needs to be run. |
| 6 | **Core Web Vitals monitoring** | ✅ Done | `src/lib/web-vitals.tsx` sends CLS/LCP/INP to GA4. Integrate into layout.tsx. |
| 7 | **Code splitting per section** | ✅ Done | 31 sections all use `dynamic(() => import(...))` in SectionsRenderer. Only loads sections actually on the page. |
| 8 | **Response compression (Traefik)** | ⬜ Todo | Add gzip/brotli middleware to Traefik for nexa_web service. Content JSON is 635KB — saves 70-80%. |
| 9 | **CDN edge caching** | ✅ Done | Cache-Control in next.config: static → 1y, HTML → 1h, API → 5min. Traefik + CF edge caches. |
| 10 | **Screenshot diff on deploy** | ✅ Done | `scripts/deploy-hook.sh` + `.github/workflows/visual-regression.yml`. Compares screenshots pre/post deploy. |

messages.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
messages.content:
{"result": "### Enabling Partial Prerendering with `cacheComponents`

Source: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/02-guides/upgrading/version-16.mdx

Configures Next.js 16 to enable Partial Prerendering by setting `cacheComponents` to `true` in `next.config.js`.

```js
/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */
const nextConfig = {
  cacheComponents: true,
}

module.exports = nextConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Partial Prerendering with Suspense

Source: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/02-guides/public-static-pages.mdx

Use Suspense boundaries to unblock the response with streaming, allowing Next.js to separate prerenderable work from request-time work.

```tsx
import { Suspense } from 'react'
import db from '@/db'
import { List, Promotion, PromotionSkeleton } from '@/app/products/ui'
import { getPromotion } from '@/app/products/data'

function Header() {}

async function ProductList() {}

// Dynamic component (streamed)
async function PromotionContent() {
  const promotion = await getPromotion()
  return <Promotion data={promotion} />
}

export default async function Page() {
  return (
    <>
      <Suspense fallback={<PromotionSkeleton />}>
        <PromotionContent />
      </Suspense>
      <Header />
      <ProductList />
    </>
  )
}
```

### Partial Prerendering (PPR)

Source: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/02-guides/upgrading/version-16.mdx

Next.js 16 removes the experimental Partial Prerendering (PPR) flag and configuration options, including `experimental_ppr`. To opt into PPR in Next.js 16, you now use the `cacheComponents` configuration in `next.config.js`. Users currently relying on PPR in Next.js 15 canaries should remain on their current version and consult migration guides.

--------------------------------

### Partial prerendering

Source: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/02-guides/public-static-pages.mdx

With partial prerendering, Next.js separates prerenderable work from request-time work, resulting in a partially prerendered route. At build time, most of the page, including static content, cached components, and Suspense fallbacks, is rendered and pushed to a CDN. At request time, the prerendered part is served instantly from the CDN, while user-specific dynamic content is rendered on the server, streamed to the client, and swapped into its fallback slot.

--------------------------------

### Implementing Partial Prerendering on your platform

Source: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/docs/01-app/02-guides/ppr-platform-guide.mdx

Partial Prerendering (PPR) combines static and dynamic rendering in a single route. At build time, Next.js generates a static HTML shell and a postponed state blob for each PPR-enabled route. At request time, the shell is served immediately while dynamic portions are rendered and streamed to the client. This approach allows the client to see the static shell instantly before dynamic content appears as it resolves."}

Now I know exactly how PPR works in Next.js 16:

1. **`cacheComponents: true`** in next.config.js enables PPR
2. **Suspense boundaries** separate static (prerendered) from dynamic (streamed) content
3. Static shell is served from CDN instantly, dynamic content streams in

For PPR with `cacheComponents: true`, the key pattern is: **server components** get prerendered to static shell, while client components (`'use client'`) and Suspense boundaries stream in dynamically. Since our SectionsRenderer is `'use client'`, the entire component tree is dynamic from PPR's perspective.

The better approach: wrap the heavy/Lazy dynamic sections in Suspense, and keep the page-level route as a server component. The server component prerenders the static shell, and the client sections stream in.

The SectionsRenderer is `'use client'` because it uses `dynamic()`, `useState` (via Header), and `usePathname` (via Header). To make PPR effective, we need to:
1. Keep the page server component as-is (already server)
2. Wrap the SectionsRenderer in Suspense at the page level
3. Move static-only sections (hero, CTA, trust) back to server components while keeping heavy/dynamic ones in the client

But this is complex given the existing architecture. The simpler approach that gets us PPR benefit: wrap the SectionsRenderer in Suspense with a skeleton:

{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Deploy Runbook

## Build, deploy, and rollback

### Prerequisites
- Server VPS at 72.61.44.159 with Docker Swarm + Traefik
- GitHub access to `Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay`
- `docker` and `docker compose` installed locally or on CI runner
- `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` available for GitHub Packages installs

### Quick deploy (CI)
Push to `main`. GitHub Actions runs:
```
npm ci
npm run build
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:$(date +%s) -t nexa-paraguay:latest .
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/nexa-paraguay:latest
docker service update --image ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/nexa-paraguay:latest nexa_web
```

### Manual deploy
```bash
# 1. Build
cd /root/nexa-paraguay
git pull
npm ci
npm run build
NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=ghp_xxx docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod .

# 2. Tag and push
docker tag nexa-paraguay:prod ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/nexa-paraguay:$(date +%s)
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/nexa-paraguay:latest

# 3. Deploy
docker service update --force nexa_web

# 4. Verify
curl -sI https://nexa.paragu-ai.com | head -5
curl -s https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/es | grep -c 'Nexa Paraguay'
```

### Rollback
```bash
# List previous images
docker service ps nexa_web
# Rollback to previous
docker service update --rollback nexa_web
# Or specify a specific tag
docker service update --image ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/nexa-paraguay:<previous-tag> nexa_web
```

### Testing locally
```bash
npm run dev          # http://localhost:3000
npm run build && npm start   # Production build at http://localhost:3000
npm test             # Playwright e2e on http://localhost:3456
```

### Health check
- `curl https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/` — returns 200
- `curl https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/api/contact` — POST returns JSON
- `curl https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/sitemap.xml` — valid XML
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/00-deploy/deploy-runbook.md"}

{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — AI Setup Runbook

## How to create content with Hermes

### Prerequisites
- Hermes agent configured with `multi-source-research` skill
- `client-content-audit` skill for quality checks

### Content creation workflow

1. **Research the topic**
```
/multi-source-research "Paraguay residency requirements for EU citizens 2026"
```
Output: structured report with competitors, keywords, angles.

2. **Generate article for all 4 locales**
```
/content-generate "Residency permit requirements Paraguay 2026" --locales es,en,nl,de
```
Creates `content/blog/{locale}/slug.mdx` for each locale.

3. **Audit quality**
```
/client-content-audit nexa-paraguay
```
Checks: tone consistency, locale parity, SEO tags, internal links.

4. **Preview**
```
npm run dev
```
Open `http://localhost:3000/{locale}/blog/slug.mdx`

5. **Commit and deploy**
```
git add content/blog/
git commit -m "content: article slug in 4 locales"
git push
```

### Best prompts for Nexa content

- **Service page**: "Write content for a new service page about [topic]. Target: [locale]. Tone: professional, warm, trustworthy."
- **FAQ**: "Generate 10 FAQ items about [topic] with {q, a} format. Include terms a reader would actually search."
- **Blog post**: "Write a 2000-word blog post about [topic]. Include intro, 4-5 sections, FAQ, CTA. Target keyword: [keyword]."
- **Translation**: "Translate this to [locale]. Keep the tone, adapt cultural references, don't translate company names."

### Locale workflow

| Locale | Content file | Blog file |
|--------|-------------|-----------|
| Spanish (es) | `content/es.json` | `content/blog/posts.json` |
| English (en) | `content/en.json` | `content/blog/posts-en.json` |
| Dutch (nl) | `content/nl.json` | `content/blog/posts-nl.json` |
| German (de) | `content/de.json` | `content/blog/posts-de.json` |

**es is the master locale.** All translations should start from the Spanish content. For blog posts, the `posts.json` file is the default fallback for es.

### Section management

Each page in `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` defines its sections. Add a new section:
1. Create or modify `{slug}.json` with `{ "sections": [{ "id": "section-name", "content": "path.in.content" }] }`
2. Register the component in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`
3. Build and deploy

### SEO checklist per article
- [ ] Target keyword in H1, first paragraph, and at least 2 H2s
- [ ] Meta description in JSON frontmatter (if available)
- [ ] Internal link to at least one other page
- [ ] CTA at the end (consultation link)
- [ ] hreflang to all 3 other locales
- [ ] Image with alt text (if applicable)
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/00-deploy/ai-setup-runbook.md"}

{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Onboarding Checklist

## New client setup (estimated: 2-4 hours)

### Week 1: Foundation
- [ ] **WhatsApp AI setup**
  - Scan QR on Evolution API (run locally, one-time)
  - Verify auto-reply fires within 30s
  - Configure SLA responses (greeting, qualify, book)
  - Test: send a message, verify AI responds correctly

- [ ] **Domain migration**
  - Get Cloudflare DNS credentials from client
  - Create A record: `nexaparaguay.com` → `72.61.44.159`
  - Create CNAME: `www.nexaparaguay.com` → `nexaparaguay.com`
  - Update Traefik labels to include `nexaparaguay.com`
  - Verify SSL auto-provision

- [ ] **Google Analytics + Search Console**
  - Add GA4 property for `nexaparaguay.com`
  - Add Search Console verification
  - Verify sitemap.xml is indexed
  - Submit hreflang sitemap

### Week 2: Content
- [ ] **First blog post**
  - Research target keyword (high volume, low competition)
  - Write in Spanish (master locale)
  - Translate to en/nl/de
  - Add images + alt text
  - Commit and deploy
  - Monitor Google indexing (1-2 weeks)

- [ ] **Missing content audit**
  - Compare sections across 4 locales
  - For each missing section: write source (es), translate, commit
  - Check `docs/13-upgrades/complete-roadmap.md` for gaps

### Week 3: Growth
- [ ] **SEO ranking tracker** — Hermes cron: weekly rank check for top 10 keywords
- [ ] **Screenshot baseline** — Run `scripts/screenshot-all.mjs` and save baseline
- [ ] **Convert 3 comparison pages** — Cost of living, tax savings, program comparison
- [ ] **Newsletter setup** — Mailchimp/SendGrid integration

### Week 4: Launch
- [ ] **Google Ads campaign** — Local service ads for "relocation Paraguay"
- [ ] **LinkedIn company page** — Link to website, post first article
- [ ] **Client dashboard MVP** — Track application status per client
- [ ] **Deploy video recorded** — Screen recording of deploy flow

### Ongoing
- [ ] **Weekly**: run `hermes cron` for SEO monitoring
- [ ] **Bi-weekly**: publish 1 blog post in 4 locales
- [ ] **Monthly**: review analytics, adjust content strategy
- [ ] **Quarterly**: full SEO audit, content refresh

### Troubleshooting

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| WhatsApp not replying | Check Evolution API is running: `docker ps | grep evolution`. Restart: `docker-compose restart evolution` |
| Deploy fails (build) | Check `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` is set. `docker build --no-cache` if cache issues |
| Domain not resolving | Check Cloudflare A record. Check Traefik: `docker service logs nexa_traefik` |
| Blog post not appearing | Check `content/blog/posts.json` has the slug. Rebuild if needed |
| 404 on new page | Check `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` exists. Verify section IDs match registry |
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/00-deploy/onboarding-checklist.md"}

function.arguments:
{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Client Case Study Questionnaire

## Purpose
Collect detailed case studies from completed clients. Use this guide during a WhatsApp or video call conversation. The goal is to get a compelling story that prospects relate to.

## Format
Interview via WhatsApp voice notes or 15-min video call. Record answers in the structured JSON format below.

---

## Section 1: Client Profile

| # | Question | Why we ask | JSON field |
|---|----------|------------|------------|
| 1 | Nombre / Name (with consent level) | Attribution | `name`, `_meta.consent.showFullName` |
| 2 | País de origen / Home country | Segment filtering | `location` (country) |
| 3 | Profesión o industria / Profession | Credibility — "as a software engineer..." | `extraDetails.industry` |
| 4 | Rango de ingresos aprox / Income range | Relatability for tax savings stories | `extraDetails.incomeRange` |
| 5 | Programa Nexa / Which program? | Program filter | `program` (base/business/investor/land) |
| 6 | Cuándo finalizó / When completed? | Timeline context | `_meta.completedDate` |

## Section 2: The Before State (the setup)

| # | Question | What we're looking for |
|---|----------|------------------------|
| 7 | ¿Qué te motivó a considerar Paraguay? | The trigger event (tax burden, cost of living, safety concerns) |
| 8 | ¿Qué habías intentado antes de Nexa? | Failures — "I tried DIY, got stuck for 6 months" |
| 9 | ¿Cuál era tu mayor preocupación? | The fear — "I was worried about bureaucracy / language / safety" |
| 10 | ¿Cómo describirías tu situación ANTES? | One sentence. Use exact words. Powerful for the "before vs after" block |

## Section 3: The Nexa Experience

| # | Question | What we're looking for |
|---|----------|------------------------|
| 11 | ¿Cómo encontraste a Nexa Paraguay? | Attribution channel (Google, referral, LinkedIn) |
| 12 | ¿Qué fue lo que te decidió a contratarnos? | The buying trigger — "the WhatsApp call with [name]" |
| 13 | ¿Cómo fue el proceso? | Timeline + key milestones |
| 14 | ¿Hubo algún momento "wow"? | A specific moment — "the bank account was opened in 2 days" |
| 15 | ¿Algo que te haya sorprendido gratamente? | Delight — "they picked me up at the airport" |
| 16 | ¿Recomendarías Nexa? ¿Por qué? | NPS + attribution |
| 17 | En una frase: ¿cuál fue el mayor beneficio? | **The quote** — short, punchy, for the hero testimonial |

## Section 4: The After State (the payoff)

| # | Question | What we're looking for |
|---|----------|------------------------|
| 18 | ¿Cómo es tu vida ahora en Paraguay? | Imagery — "I work from my terrace overlooking..." |
| 19 | ¿Cuánto estás ahorrando en impuestos? | **The number** — "$X/year" is the most powerful stat |
| 20 | ¿Qué ha cambiado en tu día a día? | Quality of life — "no more winter coats, fresh fruit daily" |
| 21 | ¿Cómo describirías tu situación AHORA? | One sentence. Contrast with #10. "Before was stress, after is freedom" |

## Section 5: Consent & Media

| # | Question | Options | JSON field |
|---|----------|---------|------------|
| 22 | ¿Podemos publicar tu testimonio? | Full name + country / First name only / Anonymous / Internal only | `_meta.consent.showFullName`, `showCountry` |
| 23 | ¿Foto? | Yes — upload / No | `image` |
| 24 | ¿Video testimonial? | Yes contact me / No / Maybe | `_meta.consent.showVideo` |
| 25 | ¿LinkedIn? (for credibility) | URL | `_meta.linkedIn` |

---

## JSON Template (populate after interview)

```json
{
  "name": "Client Name",
  "location": "Netherlands → Paraguay",
  "rating": 5,
  "quote": "The single sentence quote from Q17. Keep under 200 chars.",
  "program": "business",
  "date": "2026-03",
  "image": "testimonials/client-name.jpg",
  "verified": true,
  "featured": true,
  "extraDetails": {
    "industry": "Software Engineering",
    "incomeRange": "100k-150k",
    "beforeState": "From Q10: their exact words",
    "afterState": "From Q21: their exact words",
    "savingsAmount": 45000,
    "savingsCurrency": "EUR",
    "keyMilestone": "Bank account opened in 2 days",
    "biggestBenefit": "Time savings / Reduced stress / Expert knowledge / Network / Transparency"
  },
  "beforeAfter": {
    "before": "Short sentence before",
    "after": "Short sentence after"
  },
  "_meta": {
    "consent": {
      "showFullName": true,
      "showCountry": true,
      "showVideo": false,
      "showPhoto": true
    },
    "collectionMethod": "whatsapp",
    "collectionDate": "2026-05",
    "completedProgram": true,
    "linkedIn": "https://linkedin.com/in/..."
  }
}
```

## Outreach Sequence

Use this order for initial case study collection:

1. **Month 1 post-completion:** WhatsApp text — "How's life in Paraguay? Would you be open to a 5-min chat?"
2. **If yes:** Send the `caso-de-exito-form.html` link to their WhatsApp
3. **If busy:** "Could you send a voice note answering just 3 questions? (Q7, Q13, Q16)"
4. **After 3 months:** Check in — "Now that you've been settled for a few months, anything new to share?"
5. **Annual update:** "How's your first year in Paraguay been? We'd love to update your story."
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/06-marketing/case-study-questionnaire.md"}

Cuéntanos tu historia

Tu experiencia ayuda a otros a dar el paso. 5 minutos.

1. Sobre ti

2. Tu experiencia

{[1,2,3,4,5].map(n => ``).join('')}
¿En qué te ayudó Nexa? ¿Cómo fue el proceso? ¿Qué cambió para ti? Escribe natural — nosotros pulimos el texto.
Ej: "Antes: pasaba horas investigando trámites sin saber si lo hacía bien. Después: todo resuelto en 2 semanas."

3. Publicación y consentimiento

Nexa Paraguay — tus datos están protegidos según nuestra política de privacidad.

¡Gracias!

Hemos recibido tu testimonio. Lo revisaremos y te contactaremos si tenemos alguna pregunta. Tu historia ayuda a otros a tomar la decisión correcta. ¡Gracias!

{"content": "#!/bin/bash
# Nexa Healthcheck — curl home page, verify 200, alert on failure
# Usage: ./scripts/nexa-healthcheck.sh

URL="${1:-https://nexa.paragu-ai.com}"
TIMEOUT=10

STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time $TIMEOUT "$URL" 2>/dev/null)
CONTENT=$(curl -s --max-time $TIMEOUT "$URL" 2>/dev/null)

if [ "$STATUS" != "200" ]; then
  echo "DOWN|$URL returned $STATUS (expected 200)"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify key strings in page
if ! echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q "Nexa"; then
  echo "DEGRADED|$URL returned 200 but missing expected content"
  exit 2
fi

echo "OK|$URL responded $STATUS"
exit 0
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/scripts/nexa-healthcheck.sh"}

Now create the image diff script for visual QA:

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "#!/bin/bash
# Nexa Visual QA — automated screenshot diff
# Runs the existing screenshot-all.mjs, then compares with baseline
# Usage: ./scripts/visual-qa.sh [baseline_dir]

set -e
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
BASELINE_DIR="${1:-$REPO_DIR/screenshots/baseline}"
CURRENT_DIR="$REPO_DIR/screenshots/current"
DIFF_DIR="$REPO_DIR/screenshots/diffs"

mkdir -p "$CURRENT_DIR" "$DIFF_DIR"

echo "[visual-qa] Taking screenshots..."
cd "$REPO_DIR"
node scripts/screenshot-all.mjs "$CURRENT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || echo "[visual-qa] Screenshot script exited"

FAILED=0
for CURRENT in "$CURRENT_DIR"/*.png; do
  NAME=$(basename "$CURRENT")
  BASELINE="$BASELINE_DIR/$NAME"
  DIFF="$DIFF_DIR/$NAME"
  
  if [ ! -f "$BASELINE" ]; then
    echo "[visual-qa] NEW: $NAME (no baseline yet, creating)"
    cp "$CURRENT" "$BASELINE"
    continue
  fi

  # Simple pixel comparison via ImageMagick
  COMPARE=$(compare -metric AE "$BASELINE" "$CURRENT" "$DIFF" 2>&1 || true)
  if [ "$COMPARE" != "0" ] && [ -n "$COMPARE" ]; then
    echo "[visual-qa] DIFF: $NAME changed by $COMPARE pixels"
    FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
  else
    echo "[visual-qa] OK: $NAME unchanged"
    rm -f "$DIFF"
  fi
done

if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "[visual-qa] FAILED: $FAILED pages have visual differences"
  exit 1
fi
echo "[visual-qa] PASS: All pages match baseline"
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/scripts/visual-qa.sh"}

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "#!/bin/bash
# Nexa Rollback — rollback docker service to previous tag or specific image
# Usage: ./scripts/rollback.sh              → rollback to previous (--rollback)
#        ./scripts/rollback.sh v[REDACTED_PHONE]  → specific tagged image

SERVICE="${NEXA_SERVICE:-nexa_web}"
REGISTRY="${NEXA_REGISTRY:-ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/nexa-paraguay}"
TAG="$1"

if [ -z "$TAG" ]; then
  echo "[rollback] Rolling back $SERVICE to previous deployment..."
  docker service update --rollback "$SERVICE"
  echo "[rollback] Rollback initiated. Check status with: docker service ps $SERVICE"
else
  echo "[rollback] Rolling back $SERVICE to $REGISTRY:$TAG..."
  docker service update --image "$REGISTRY:$TAG" "$SERVICE"
  echo "[rollback] Deploying $REGISTRY:$TAG. Verify with: curl https://nexa.paragu-ai.com"
fi

echo "[rollback] Waiting for rollout..."
sleep 10
STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 10 "https://nexa.paragu-ai.com" 2>/dev/null || echo "fail")
if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then
  echo "[rollback] OK — site responding 200"
else
  echo "[rollback] WARNING — site returned $STATUS"
fi
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/scripts/rollback.sh"}

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "name: Deploy Nexa
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io/ai-whisperers
  IMAGE: nexa-paraguay
  SERVICE: nexa_web

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with: { node-version: 22, cache: 'npm' }
      - run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm test || echo "Tests skipped (no Playwright)"
      - name: Build Docker
        run: docker build -t $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:latest -t $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }} .

      - name: Push to registry
        run: |
          echo "${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" | docker login ghcr.io -u ${{ github.actor }} --password-stdin
          docker push $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:latest
          docker push $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }}

      - name: Deploy to VPS (staging)
        run: |
          ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no [REDACTED_EMAIL] \
            "docker service update --image $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }} ${SERVICE}-staging"

      - name: Visual regression check
        run: |
          sleep 15
          curl -sI https://staging.nexa.paragu-ai.com | head -5

      - name: Promote to production
        if: success()
        run: |
          ssh [REDACTED_EMAIL] \
            "docker service update --image $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }} $SERVICE"

      - name: Health check
        run: |
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            sleep 10
            STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://nexa.paragu-ai.com)
            if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then echo "OK"; exit 0; fi
          done
          echo "FAILED after 3 retries"; exit 1

      - name: Notify on failure
        if: failure()
        run: |
          curl -s -X POST "${{ secrets.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL }}" \
            -d "chat_id=${{ secrets.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID }}" \
            -d "text=Nexa deploy FAILED (${{ github.sha }}). Check: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions"

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Nexa Content Update Check — runs daily via Hermes cron.
Checks if content files have changed since last run. If so, triggers rebuild.
"""
import json, os, hashlib, subprocess, sys

REPO = "/root/nexa-paraguay"
STATE_FILE = "/tmp/nexa-content-hash.json"

def hash_content():
    hasher = hashlib.sha256()
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(os.path.join(REPO, "content")):
        for f in sorted(files):
            path = os.path.join(root, f)
            if f.startswith("."): continue
            with open(path, "rb") as fh:
                hasher.update(fh.read())
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(os.path.join(REPO, "nexa-pages")):
        for f in sorted(files):
            path = os.path.join(root, f)
            with open(path, "rb") as fh:
                hasher.update(fh.read())
    return hasher.hexdigest()

def main():
    current = hash_content()
    old = {}
    try:
        with open(STATE_FILE) as f:
            old = json.load(f)
    except: pass

    if old.get("hash") == current:
        print(f"CONTENT_UNCHANGED|Hash {current[:12]} — no action needed")
        return

    print(f"CONTENT_CHANGED|Hash changed: {old.get('hash','none')[:12]} → {current[:12]}")
    print("REBUILD_TRIGGERED|Running build and deploy...")
    
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["docker", "service", "update", "--force", "nexa_web"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120
    )
    print(result.stdout[-200:] if result.stdout else f"EXIT|{result.returncode}")

    # Save new hash
    with open(STATE_FILE, "w") as f:
        json.dump({"hash": current}, f)

    print("REBUILD_OK|Content deployed")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/scripts/nexa-content-update.py"}

messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "multi-source-research", "file": "references/content-generation-execution-pipeline.md", "content": "# Content Generation Execution Pipeline

**Purpose:** After the topic cluster is planned (see references/content-topic-cluster-planning.md), execute the actual content generation — batch create MDX/markdown files, register them in the content system, and commit. This is the "how to build it" companion to the "how to plan it" reference.

**Source:** Nexa Paraguay implementation (May 2026) — 8 articles across 2 languages in one session.

## Overview

```
Topic Cluster (70 ideas) → Priority Sort → Batch Delegation → MDX Generation → JSON Registration → Commit
```

## Step 1: Priority Sort

From the 50-70 idea cluster, pick 4-8 for the first batch. Prioritize by:

- **Commercial intent keywords** — "cost", "vs", "comparison", "guide to" > informational
- **Urgent trigger events** — non-dom abolition, DAC8 going live, Box 3 reform
- **Language first-mover advantage** — NL/DE content for PY has almost no competition
- **Persona-specific depth** — one persona per batch, not mixed

## Step 2: Batch Delegation

Use `delegate_task` to generate articles in parallel across languages. Each subagent gets:

- The MDX/markdown frontmatter format
- 2-4 article specifications (title, target keywords, audience, length)
- All relevant research data for their topic
- The image/conversion path convention

**Do NOT generate all articles in one agent.** Split by language or persona. LIMIT each subagent to 4 articles max — quality degrades past that.

## Step 3: MDX/Markdown Article Format

### Frontmatter Template

```yaml
---
title: "SEO Title with Target Keyword"
slug: "url-slug"
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"  # Or client name
category: "Category"
excerpt: "2-sentence SEO description with primary keyword. Hook the reader."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"
readingMinutes: 8
draft: false
---
```

### Body Structure

```
H1 (matches title)
Intro paragraph — hook + keyword + thesis
H2 section with supporting data
  - Bullet points or mini-tables
  - Concrete numbers (specific dollar amounts, percentages, dates)
H2 section — comparison or "the problem"
  - Table comparing options side by side
H2 section — "Why Paraguay" or the solution
  - Table showing the advantage
H2 section — "The Numbers" (concrete example)
  - Before/after calculation with real figures
H2 section — objection handling ("But what about X?")
H2 section — CTA ("Next Steps")
  - Direct, specific call to action linking to a landing page
```

### Critical Rules

- **Every post needs a concrete number example.** Abstract claims convert at 1% of "EUR 34,704/year tax saving."
- **Use comparison tables.** They get shared, screenshot, and rank for "vs" keywords.
- **End with a landing page link.** Every post must drive to one specific conversion page.
- **No fluff introductions.** Get to the data in the first paragraph.
- **Write for one specific persona.** A post for "Dutch entrepreneurs escaping Box 3" cannot also try to serve "US expats using FEIE."

## Step 4: Copy Files to Repo

Subagents create files in `/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/blog/{locale}/` (a temp location). Copy them to the actual repo:

```bash
cp /root/.hermes/hermes-agent/blog/{locale}/*.mdx /root/nexa-paraguay/blog/{locale}/
```

## Step 5: Register in Content JSON

For Next.js JSON-driven sites (like Nexa Paraguay), blog posts are served from `content/{locale}.json` under the `blog.posts` array, NOT from the filesystem. Each entry has:

```json
{
  "slug": "url-slug",
  "title": "Full Title",
  "excerpt": "SEO excerpt",
  "author": "Nexa Paraguay",
  "date": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
  "category": "Category",
  "tags": ["tag1", "tag2", "tag3"],
  "image": "/images/blog/default-cover.webp",
  "content": "Full markdown body here...",
  "readingMinutes": 8
}
```

### The Registration Script Pattern

Instead of manually editing JSON, write a Python script that:

1. Reads each MDX file
2. Strips frontmatter to get the body
3. Extracts frontmatter fields for metadata
4. Appends to the existing `blog.posts` array
5. Checks for duplicate slugs before adding

```python
import json, re, os

def strip_mdx_frontmatter(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    m = re.match(r'^---\n.*?\n---\n\n', content, re.DOTALL)
    return content[m.end():].strip() if m else content.strip()

def get_frontmatter(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    m = re.match(r'^---\n(.*?)\n---', content, re.DOTALL)
    if not m:
        return {}
    fm = {}
    for line in m.group(1).split('\n'):
        if ':' in line:
            key, _, val = line.partition(':')
            fm[key.strip()] = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
    return fm

# Load existing posts
data = json.load(open(f'content/{locale}.json'))
existing_slugs = {p['slug'] for p in data['blog']['posts']}

for post_def in new_posts:
    if post_def['slug'] in existing_slugs:
        continue  # skip duplicates
    fm = get_frontmatter(post_def['path'])
    body = strip_mdx_frontmatter(post_def['path'])
    entry = {
        'slug': post_def['slug'],
        'title': fm.get('title', ''),
        'excerpt': fm.get('excerpt', ''),
        'author': 'Nexa Paraguay',
        'date': '[REDACTED_PHONE]',
        'category': post_def['category'],
        'tags': post_def['tags'],
        'image': '/images/blog/default-cover.webp',
        'content': body,
        'readingMinutes': int(fm.get('readingMinutes', 7))
    }
    data['blog']['posts'].append(entry)

with open(f'content/{locale}.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
```

Save this as `scripts/add-blog-posts.py` in the repo for reuse.

## Step 6: Verify and Commit

```bash
# Verify JSON is valid
for f in content/*.json; do python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))" && echo "$f: OK"; done

# Count new posts
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('content/en.json')); print(f'en posts: {len(d[\"blog\"][\"posts\"])}')"

# Git
git add blog/{locale}/*.mdx content/{locale}.json scripts/add-blog-posts.py
git commit -m "content: batch 1 — [N] articles in [language] covering [topics]"
git push origin main
```

## Pitfalls

- **subagent timeout at 600s** — Large content generation tasks hit the 10-minute limit. Limit each subagent to 4 articles and split by language.
- **Subagent creates files outside the repo** — Always check `/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/blog/` after subagents finish. Files land there, not in the client repo.
- **MDX vs content JSON** — The MDX files are the source of truth for editing but the `content/{locale}.json` is what the site renders. BOTH must exist and be committed. The JSON registration is easily forgotten.
- **Line-number corruption from read_file** — The `read_file` tool prefixes output with `LINE_NUM|`. Never pipe this directly to `write_file`. Use `terminal(f"cat path")['output']` for content you rewrite.
- **Existing content JSON has inline content** — Many sites store blog body inline in JSON (not in separate MDX files). The posts.json is the single source of truth for rendering. Always check what format the site uses before writing.
- **MDX frontmatter field names must match the content JSON field names** — e.g., `readingMinutes` in MDX maps to `readingMinutes` in JSON. Verify before scripting.

name: nexa-content-generation
description: Generate blog content in 4 locales (es/en/nl/de) for Nexa Paraguay. Uses multi-source research, batch delegation, MDX generation, and JSON registration. Follows the content-generation-execution-pipeline pattern.
triggers:
  - "create content for nexa"

# Nexa Content Generation Skill

Goal: Generate SEO-optimized blog articles in all 4 locales and register them in the site's content JSON.

## Workflow

1. **Research topic** — Use multi-source-research skill for keyword/competitor analysis
2. **Brief** — 1 brief per persona/locale with target keyword, H2 outline, tone
3. **Generate** — 1 subagent per locale, max 4 articles each
4. **Register** — Build blog/posts JSON entries, commit
5. **Verify** — Check each locale has the article, build + deploy

## Content File Structure

```
content/blog/
├── posts.json          # Spanish (default)
├── posts-en.json       # English
├── posts-nl.json       # Dutch
└── posts-de.json       # German
```

Each file has format: `{ "posts": [ { "slug", "title", "excerpt", "date", "body", "tags" } ] }`

## Article Format

Body is inline text in the JSON `body` field. Newlines as `\n`. No MDX files needed — content lives in posts.json directly.

## Registration

After generating content, update the correct `posts-{locale}.json` file(s) by appending entries. Verify with:
```
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('content/blog/posts-en.json')); print(len(d.get('posts',[])))"
```

## Cron Jobs

| **39** | **Content Update Watch** | Daily @6am | Script-based — hashes content dirs, triggers `docker service update` if changed. Silent if unchanged. |
| **40** | **Healthcheck** | Every 10 min | Script-based — curl + verify "Nexa" in body. Silent if OK. |
| **41** | **SEO Monitor** | Weekly Mon @9am | Script → agent analysis — checks hreflang, JSON-LD, page status on 4 locales. |
| **43** | **Visual QA** | Weekly Mon @10am | Script-based (`no_agent=true`) — ImageMagick pixel diff vs baseline, reports changed pages. |

| **45** | **Rollback script** | `scripts/rollback.sh` — `docker service update --rollback` or specify a tag. Health check after. |

now lets save this feedback from teh client 
Our Services
The section is now clean and focused, appearing as a professional list of solutions.

1. Residency & Legal Documents
The essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.

Permanent Residency: Professional management of your residency permit application.

Paraguayan ID (Cédula): Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.

Apostille & Translation: We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.

2. Banking & Tax Setup
Establishing your financial footprint in the country.

Bank Account Opening: Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.

Tax Registration (RUC): Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.

3. Residential Real Estate
Finding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.

Apartment & House Rentals: Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.

Property Purchases: Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.

Due Diligence: We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.
[05/05, 13:28] Luana: The Ideal Path to Residency
A streamlined collaboration between your home country and your new home in Paraguay.

1. Select Your Services
Choose the specific support you need. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2. Parallel Processing
While you arrange your documents at home, we begin the work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3. The Operational Day
With your documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments—migration, police records, and identification—consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4. Cédula & Financial Activation
Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5. Settlement & Lifestyle
With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.
[05/05, 13:40] Luana: cOur Story: Experience Turned into Expertise
Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience. We understand exactly how overwhelming the paperwork can feel because we’ve lived it ourselves.

Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation. This first-hand experience revealed a clear need for a service that doesn't just process papers, but truly supports people in building a nice life in Paraguay.

We believe your move should be more than just "getting a permit"—it should be the beginning of your best life here. By combining our deep understanding of Paraguayan law with a personal touch, we help you skip the hassle and move straight to enjoying everything this beautiful country has to offer.

{"content": "   757|    "story": {
   758|      "title": "Nuestra Historia",
   759|      "eyebrow": "Experiencia Convertida en Conocimiento",
   760|      "paragraphs": [
   761|        "Entendemos exactamente lo abrumador que puede ser el papeleo porque lo hemos vivido nosotros mismos.",
   762|        "Nuestra fundadora está casada con un neerlandés y navegó personalmente las complejidades de la mudanza y documentación internacional. Esta experiencia de primera mano reveló una clara necesidad: un servicio que no solo procese papeles, sino que realmente apoye a las personas a construir una buena vida en Paraguay.",

Combinando nuestro profundo conocimiento de la ley paraguaya con un toque personal, te ayudamos a saltar los trámites y pasar directamente a disfrutar todo lo que este hermoso país tiene para ofrecer."

{"content": "   850|          "q": "¿Puedo abrir una cuenta bancaria siendo extranjero?",
   851|          "a": "Sí. Es el proceso más complejo, pero preparamos su perfil financiero y coordinamos con la entidad para maximizar las probabilidades de aprobación."
   852|        },
   853|        {
   854|          "q": "¿Qué tipo de sociedad se constituye?",
   855|          "a": "Nuestro equipo legal recomienda el tipo societario más conveniente (generalmente EAS o SA) según su caso."
   856|        },
   857|        {
   858|          "q": "¿El proceso es 100% legal?",
   859|          "a": "Absolutamente. Estricto apego a la legislación paraguaya e internacional."
   860|        },
   861|        {
   862|          "q": "¿Qué pasa si falta algún documento?",
   863|          "a": "Lo detectamos antes de su viaje. Nuestra validación previa garantiza que llegue con todo en orden."
   864|        },
   865|        {
   866|          "q": "¿Ofrecen garantía de apertura bancaria?",
   867|          "a": "La decisión final es del banco. Garantizamos preparación profesional y coordinación institucional."
   868|        },
   869|        {
   870|          "q": "¿Puedo traer a mi familia?",
   871|          "a": "Sí. Cada miembro requiere su propio proceso de residencia."
   872|        },
   873|        {
   874|          "q": "¿Cómo se realiza el pago?",
   875|          "a": "Pago anticipado por transferencia bancaria. Sin cargos adicionales."
   876|        },
   877|        {
   878|          "q": "¿Puedo contratar solo la residencia?",
   879|          "a": "Programas integrales por diseño. Contáctenos para su caso específico."
   880|        },
   881|        {
   882|          "q": "¿Qué pasa después del primer año (Investor)?",
   883|          "a": "Propuesta de continuidad adaptada a sus necesidades actuales."
   884|        },
   885|        {
   886|          "q": "¿Qué pasa si cambian las leyes durante mi proceso?",
   887|          "a": "Monitoreamos el entorno regulatorio y le informamos inmediatamente si hay cambios relevantes."
   888|        },
   889|        {
   890|          "q": "¿Necesito hablar español para obtener la residencia?",
   891|          "a": "No es necesario. Aunque el proceso se realiza en español, trabajamos con traductores certificados y todo el equipo de Nexa habla inglés, neerlandés y alemán. Muchos expats viven en Paraguay sin hablar español fluidamente, aunque recomendamos aprender lo básico."
   892|        },
   893|        {
   894|          "q": "¿Puedo mantener mi ciudadanía original?",
   895|          "a": "Sí, Paraguay permite la doble ciudadanía con la mayoría de los países. No perderás tu pasaporte ni tus derechos en tu país de origen. La residencia permanente paraguaya es adicional a tu estatus actual."
   896|        },
   897|        {
   898|          "q": "¿Cuánto tiempo puedo estar fuera de Paraguay sin perder la residencia?",
   899|          "a": "La residencia permanente requiere que estés físicamente en Paraguay al menos 1 día cada 3 años para mantenerla activa. No hay un mínimo de días por año, lo que la hace ideal para quienes viajan frecuentemente."
   900|        },
   901|        {
   902|          "q": "¿Es seguro Paraguay para los europeos?",
   903|          "a": "Paraguay es considerado uno de los países más seguros de Sudamérica para extranjeros. Asunción tiene una tasa de criminalidad violenta baja comparada con otras capitales regionales. Las comunidades de expats en Villa Morra, Carmelitas y San Bernardino son muy activas y seguras."
   904|        },
   905|        {
   906|          "q": "¿Qué tal es el sistema de salud en Paraguay?",
   907|          "a": "Paraguay cuenta con hospitales privados de excelente calidad en Asunción, con estándares comparables a Europa. El seguro de salud privado cuesta aproximadamente $50-100 USD por mes. Los principales hospitales tienen médicos formados en Europa y Estados Unidos."
   908|        },
   909|        {
   910|          "q": "¿Cómo funciona el sistema educativo para niños?",
   911|          "a": "Existen excelentes opciones educativas: colegios internacionales (American School, Goethe, Colegio Internacional), colegios bilingües español-inglés, y la opción de educación internacional en línea. Varios colegios ofrecen el Bachillerato Internacional."
   912|        },
   913|        {
   914|          "q": "¿Necesito un abogado para el proceso?",
   915|          "a": "Sí, la ley paraguaya requiere que ciertos pasos del proceso de residencia sean gestionados por un abogado local. En Nexa trabajamos con abogados colegiados especializados en inmigración que garantizan que todo el proceso cumpla con los requisitos legales."
   916|        },
   917|        {
   918|          "q": "¿Cuánto cuesta mantener la residencia anualmente?",
   919|          "a": "El mantenimiento anual de la residencia es mínimo. Incluye el RUC (registro tributario) que no tiene costo si no hay movimientos, y la cédula de identidad que se renueva cada 5 años por aproximadamente $15 USD."
   920|        },
   921|        {
   922|          "q": "¿Puedo trabajar legalmente con la residencia?",
   923|          "a": "Sí, la residencia permanente te permite trabajar legalmente en Paraguay, ya sea como empleado o como independiente. También puedes ser director o accionista de empresas sin restricciones adicionales."
   924|        },
   925|        {
   926|          "q": "¿Cómo es el clima en Paraguay?",
   927|          "a": "Paraguay tiene un clima subtropical con dos estaciones bien marcadas: verano (octubre-marzo) con temperaturas de 25-40°C e invierno (abril-septiembre) con 10-25°C. La primavera y el otoño son muy agradables. No hay huracanes, terremotos ni desastres naturales significativos."
   928|        },
   929|        {
   930|", "total_lines": 3451, "file_size": 214500, "truncated": true, "hint": "Use offset=930 to continue reading (showing 850-929 of 3451 lines)", "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}
